channel.title: bsd on IT notes
channel.description: Recent content in bsd on IT notes

item.title: sed append
item.description: To append a line using sed that works on macOS: sed -e '/^telegraf_enab.*/a\'$'\n''telegraf_user="root"' If need to do it in multiple files, you could use find: find . -name "rc.conf" -exec sed -i '' -e '/^telegraf_enab.*/a\'$'\n''telegraf_user="root"' {} \; https://stackoverflow.com/a/48406504/1135424
